Title: Buying requires passing GO
RuleDescription: You can't buy a property until you've passed GO at least once. Land on an unowned property before then and nothing happens — it isn't bought or auctioned, and it stays with the bank.

Title: No rent while the owner is in jail
RuleDescription: A property owner who is in jail can't collect rent. Land on their property while they're jailed and you pay nothing.

Title: Fines go to Free Parking
RuleDescription: Fines — including tax and the jail fee — are paid into the Free Parking pot, not to the bank.

Title: The third die moves everyone else
RuleDescription: After you've moved, the third die decides how far every other player moves, in clockwise order. They each act on the space they land on.

Title: Someone rolled your dice number
RuleDescription: When another player rolls your personal dice number, you collect £100 from the bank — plus a third card at the end of the turn.

Title: You rolled your own dice number
RuleDescription: Rolling your own dice number pays you £100 from the bank and £100 from every other player — plus a third card at the end of the turn.

Title: Direction is locked until you pass GO
RuleDescription: You can't change direction — even by rolling a double — until you've passed GO at least once. The first move off GO at the start of the game doesn't count.

Title: Double 1 — Snake Eyes
RuleDescription: Rolling a double 1 (snake eyes) pays you £500.

Title: Double 2 — everyone else misses a turn
RuleDescription: Rolling a double 2 makes every other player miss their next turn. You still take your own extra roll for the double.

Title: Double 3 — forward 3, then back 3
RuleDescription: Rolling a double 3 moves you forward 3 spaces (acting on that space), then back 3 spaces (acting on that one too).

Title: Double 4 — others move forward 4, then back 4
RuleDescription: Rolling a double 4 moves every other player forward 4 spaces, then back 4, each acting on the spaces they land on.

Title: Double 5 — you go forward 10, others back 10
RuleDescription: Rolling a double 5 moves you forward 10 spaces and every other player back 10. You get no extra roll and miss your next turn.

Title: Double 6 — back 12
RuleDescription: Rolling a double 6 moves you back 12 spaces.

Title: Doubles change your direction
RuleDescription: After rolling a double you turn around and travel the other way — unless you haven't passed GO yet, in which case your direction stays locked.

Title: Three doubles in a row sends you to jail
RuleDescription: Rolling three doubles in a row sends you straight to jail and ends your turn. The other players still take their third-die move for that roll.

Title: Triple bonus
RuleDescription: Rolling a triple pays you the triple bonus. The bonus grows by £500 every time you roll a triple.

Title: Triples move only you
RuleDescription: On a triple you move the combined total of all three dice, and no other player moves — there's no third-die movement.

Title: Triples don't change direction
RuleDescription: Unlike a double, rolling a triple does not change the direction you're facing.

Title: Three triples in a row sends you to jail
RuleDescription: Rolling three triples in a row sends you straight to jail.

Title: Passing GO clockwise
RuleDescription: Passing GO while travelling clockwise pays you £200.

Title: Passing GO anti-clockwise
RuleDescription: Passing GO while travelling anti-clockwise pays you £100 — less than a clockwise pass.

Title: Landing on GO
RuleDescription: Landing exactly on GO pays £200, whichever direction you're travelling.

Title: Go To Jail sends you to jail
RuleDescription: Landing on Go To Jail sends you straight to jail (unless a card you draw says otherwise).

Title: Roll a double to leave jail
RuleDescription: Rolling a double (or triple) gets you out of jail — you then move as normal for that roll.

Title: Three turns in jail, then you pay
RuleDescription: You can stay in jail for at most three turns. If you haven't left by then, you must pay the jail fee to leave on the third turn.

Title: The jail fee grows each turn
RuleDescription: The fee to leave jail starts at £50 and rises 50% for each turn you've spent inside (£50, then £75, then £113). It resets to £50 the next time you're jailed.

Title: Free Parking fee when there's nothing to take
RuleDescription: If the pot is empty, or you own no properties, you pay into Free Parking the difference between your two main dice times 100 (e.g. a 3 and a 5 → £200).

Title: A double pays no Free Parking fee
RuleDescription: Rolling a double or triple makes the two main dice equal, so the difference is zero — you pay nothing into Free Parking.

Title: Taking from Free Parking is capped
RuleDescription: When you take from Free Parking you collect up to £1000 from the pot — up to £2000 if you own a double hotel.

Title: You also take the properties in Free Parking
RuleDescription: When you take from Free Parking you also collect any properties currently sitting in the pot.

Title: Hand a property in to take from Free Parking
RuleDescription: To take from Free Parking you hand in one of your properties — one that isn't part of a set you've built on, and from a set you haven't handed in from before.

Title: Hand-ins are tracked per set
RuleDescription: Once you hand a property into Free Parking, you can't hand in any other property from that same set for the rest of the game. All stations count as one set; all utilities as another.

Title: Purge when you've nothing eligible to hand in
RuleDescription: If there's money to take but you have no property eligible to hand in, you purge one of your properties instead, and still take the money and any properties in Free Parking.

Title: Loans cover what you can't pay
RuleDescription: If you can't afford what you owe, you can take a loan from the bank for exactly the shortfall, rounded up to the nearest step according to the rounding rule. You don't have to mortgage first.

Title: Keep up to £200 when taking a loan
RuleDescription: When you take a loan you may keep up to £200 in cash — only money above £200 goes toward what you owe, and the loan covers the rest.

Title: Maximum of three loans
RuleDescription: You can have at most three loans outstanding at once.

Title: Loan repayment when you pass GO
RuleDescription: Each time you pass GO you repay 10% of the total you originally borrowed across your outstanding loans (never less than the smallest grid amount). Only passing GO triggers this — not Free Parking.

Title: Oldest loan first; overpayment is lost
RuleDescription: Repayments clear your oldest loan first. The full instalment is paid even if it's more than that loan needs — any overpayment is lost, not carried over to the next loan.

Title: Mortgage fee when you pass GO
RuleDescription: Each time you pass GO you pay 20% of the purchase cost of every property you still have mortgaged.

Title: A mortgaged property breaks its set's rent
RuleDescription: A mortgaged property earns no rent and stops counting toward its set — so the rest of the colour set (or a station's combined rent) drops to single rent until you unmortgage it.

Title: Nobody completes a set until everyone can
RuleDescription: No player may hold a complete colour set until every player is able to complete one. Until then, completing a set means reserving the final property instead of owning it.

Title: Reserve the property that completes your set
RuleDescription: If you land on the last property you need for a set while the reserve rule is active, you can reserve it for half its price instead of buying it.

Title: A reserved property is inert
RuleDescription: While a property is reserved it earns no rent, and no other player can buy it.

Title: Reserved properties end once everyone has a set
RuleDescription: As soon as a player breaks through to a complete set, the reservation rule is spent and no longer applies for the rest of the game.

Title: Declining or not affording sends it to auction
RuleDescription: If you land on an unowned property you're allowed to buy but decline it or can't afford it, it goes to auction for the whole table.

Title: Auctions open at half price
RuleDescription: Bidding doesn't start at zero — the minimum bid is half the property's purchase price, and the auction opens there.

Title: Last bidder standing wins
RuleDescription: The last player left in the auction wins, paying their current bid. If everyone else passes you win at the minimum — even the player who declined the property can end up forced to take it at half price.

Title: Nobody can afford it
RuleDescription: If no player can afford even the minimum bid, the auction is cancelled and the property stays with the bank.

Title: Stations cost more as you own more
RuleDescription: A station's price rises with how many you already own: £200, then £250, then £300, then £400 for your fourth.

Title: Mortgaged stations still raise the price
RuleDescription: Stations you've mortgaged still count toward the price of your next station — you can't dodge the rising cost by mortgaging. This is the opposite of rent, where a mortgaged station counts for nothing.

Title: Utility rent is a dice roll
RuleDescription: Rent for a utility isn't fixed — it's the utility's multiplier times the dice that moved you onto it.

Title: Which dice count for utility rent
RuleDescription: If you landed on the utility on your own roll, it's the sum of your two main dice. If the third die moved you there, it's that third die alone.

Title: Owning both utilities pays more
RuleDescription: Owning a single utility uses the lower multiplier; owning both uses the higher one. A mortgaged utility earns nothing and doesn't count toward the pair.

Title: Going bankrupt
RuleDescription: You go bankrupt when you can't pay what you owe and have no way left to raise it — nothing to mortgage and no loan available. You can also choose to declare bankruptcy at any time to quit.

Title: Bankrupt assets go to the bank
RuleDescription: When you go bankrupt, all your money and properties return to the bank — never to another player.

Title: A creditor is still paid
RuleDescription: If you were bankrupted by rent or a fine owed to another player, the bank pays that player the full amount.

Title: Last player standing wins
RuleDescription: The game is won by the last player left who hasn't gone bankrupt.
